          descriptors = [phrase(subj, t.predicate, t.object) for t in items]
          if not descriptors:
              continue
          # Adjust each descriptor: if it already starts with the subject, keep it;
          # otherwise prepend the subject.
          # Clean descriptors by removing any leading subject repetition
          clean_desc = []
          for d in descriptors:
              d = d.strip()
              if d.lower().startswith(subj.lower()):
                  d = d[len(subj):].strip()
              clean_desc.append(d.rstrip('.'))
          if len(clean_desc) == 1:
              sent = f"{subj} {clean_desc[0]}."
          else:
              if len(clean_desc) == 2:
                  body = f"{clean_desc[0]} and {clean_desc[1]}"
              else:
                  body = ", ".join(clean_desc[:-1]) + f", and {clean_desc[-1]}"
              sent = f"{subj} {body}."
          sentences.append(sent)

  # First, collect mission‑crew relationships so we can produce a single sentence per mission
      from collections import defaultdict
      mission_to_astronauts = defaultdict(list)
      skip_triples = set()          # triples that will be handled separately (mission/crewMembers)
  
      for t in triples:
          pred = t.predicate.lower().replace(" ", "")
          if pred == "mission":
              mission_to_astronauts[t.object].append(t.subject)
              skip_triples.add(t)
          elif pred == "crewmembers":
              mission_to_astronauts[t.subject].append(t.object)
              skip_triples.add(t)
  
      # Group the remaining triples by subject
      groups = defaultdict(list)
      for t in triples:
          if t in skip_triples:
              continue
          groups[t.subject].append(t)
  
      sentences = []
  
      # Create sentences for mission crew lists (combine all astronauts for the same mission)
